summary: Half-Step opener fuels a fire‑filled set with stellar China>Rider
blurb: The show kicks off with a marathon Mississippi Half‑Step → Franklin's Tower jam that sets the bar high. Highlights include a tight Passenger/Ramble/ Music Never Stopped sequence, a searing China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider, and a soaring second set capped by Playing In The Band and Stella Blue. Brent’s keyboards shine throughout and Jerry delivers wailing solos.
review:
October 28, 1979 at the Cape Cod Coliseum delivered one of the late‑’70s tour’s most electrifying nights. From the opening Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo that segues into a half‑step Franklin's Tower marathon, the band never lets up. Jerry Garcia’s lead work is on fire—ripping solos on the jam, soaring leads on China Cat Sunflower and Samson & Delilah, and a heartfelt ballad on Stella Blue—only a brief vocal hiccup in Franklin’s Tower is noted by a few listeners. Phil Lesh provides an X‑factor bass foundation that shines especially during the Half‑Step/Franklin's stretch and the explosive Playing In The Band. Brent Mydland’s keyboards are a force, adding texture on the jammed sequences and synth flourishes in the space segment. The second set maintains momentum with a flawless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue, a powerful Samson & Delilah, and a gorgeous close with Sugar Magnolia. Across all sources the performance is described as “non‑stop jamfest” that ranks among the tour’s top five shows.

For listeners seeking the clearest capture, the Miller soundboard (gd1979-10-28.sbd.miller.105434.flac16) offers a balanced mix with only minor glitches, preserving every nuance of Garcia’s guitar and Brent’s keys while keeping the live energy intact. Whether you’re after the iconic Half‑Step opener or the soaring second‑set jams, this recording is the definitive way to experience Cape Cod ’79.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Five of six analyses are strongly positive, praising the Half‑Step jam, China>Rider sequence, Brent's keyboards and overall band tightness; only one mixed review notes a vocal collapse and an AUD source swap, which does not outweigh the overwhelming acclaim.

best_recording.reason: High‑quality soundboard with clear, well‑balanced audio; captures all standout moments with minimal technical issues.

key_highlights: Marathon Half‑Step → Franklin's Tower opener that drives the whole show
Seamless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue and powerful Samson & Delilah
Brent Mydland’s keyboard textures shine on jams and space
Second‑set energy sustained through Playing In The Band, Stella Blue and Sugar Magnolia

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers wailing guitar tones and relentless solos, especially on the Half‑Step jam, China Cat Sunflower and Samson; a brief vocal collapse in Franklin's Tower is noted but overall his playing lifts the show.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides booming bass with standout lines during the Half‑Step/Franklin's stretch and Playing In The Band, described as an X‑factor journeyscape that powers the jams.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland is prominent throughout, delivering strong organ textures on China Cat Sunflower, synth flourishes in space, and dynamic interplay on Franklin's Tower; reviewers call him a force to be reckoned with.
